In [12]:
import tensorflow as tf
from tensorflow import keras
from keras import datasets,models,layers
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from keras.utils import to_categorical
from keras.layers import BatchNormalization,Dropout
from keras.callbacks import EarlyStopping
import random
In [13]:
with np.load("cifar-10.npz")as data:
    train_images,train_labels=data['x_train'],data['y_train']
    test_images,test_labels=data['x_test'],data['y_test']
In [14]:
max_val=max(train_images.max(),test_images.max())
print(f"max value used for normalization : {max_val}")
train_images=train_images.astype("float")/max_val
test_images=test_images.astype("float")/max_val

y_train_cat=to_categorical(train_labels, num_classes=10)
y_test_cat=to_categorical(test_labels, num_classes=10)

max value used for normalization : 255
In [20]:
print(f" shape of training images : {train_images.shape} and type : {train_images.dtype}" )
print(f"shape of training labels : {train_labels.shape} and type : {test_labels.dtype}")

 shape of training images : (50000, 32, 32, 3) and type : float64
shape of training labels : (50000, 1) and type : int64
In [29]:
class_names = ['airplane','automobile','bird','cat','deer','dog','frog','horse','ship','truck']

# Find unique labels (0–9) and their first occurrence index
unique_labels, unique_indices = np.unique(train_labels, return_index=True)

plt.figure(figsize=(15,6))
plt.suptitle("Unique Images with Labels and Class Names", fontsize=16, fontweight='bold')

for i, idx in enumerate(unique_indices):
    plt.subplot(2,5,i+1)
    img = tf.image.resize(train_images[idx], (96,96)).numpy()
    label = int(train_labels[idx][0])
    plt.imshow(np.clip(img, 0, 1))
    plt.title(f"{class_names[label]} - {label}")
    plt.axis('off')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
In [33]:
model=models.Sequential([
    #First convolution block
    layers.Conv2D(32,(3,3),activation="relu", input_shape=(32,32,3)),
    BatchNormalization(),
    layers.MaxPooling2D((2,2)),
    Dropout(0.2),
    
    #second block 
    layers.Conv2D(64,(3,3),activation="relu"),
    BatchNormalization(),
    layers.MaxPooling2D((2,2)),
    Dropout(0.2),

    #third block
    layers.Conv2D(64,(3,3),activation="tanh"),
    BatchNormalization(),

    #flatten nd dense layers
    layers.Flatten(),
    layers.Dense(64,activation="relu"),
    layers.Dense(10,activation="softmax")  
])
model.summary()

Model: "sequential_1"

In [36]:
adam_optimizer=keras.optimizers.Adam(learning_rate=0.001)

model.compile(optimizer=adam_optimizer,loss="categorical_crossentropy",metrics=["accuracy"])
In [39]:
early_stop=EarlyStopping(monitor="val_loss",patience=3, restore_best_weights=True)

history=model.fit(train_images,y_train_cat,epochs=15,validation_data=(test_images,y_test_cat),callbacks=[early_stop],batch_size=64)

In [44]:
# Pick a random test image
n = random.randint(0, len(test_images)-1)

plt.figure(figsize=(4,2))
plt.imshow(test_images[n])
plt.axis('off')
# Show predicted class name as title
predicted_label = class_names[np.argmax(predicted_values[n])]
plt.title(f"Predicted: {predicted_label}", fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
plt.show()
In [45]:
plt.figure(figsize=(8,5))
plt.plot(history.history['accuracy'], marker='o')
plt.plot(history.history['val_accuracy'], marker='s')
plt.title('MODEL ACCURACY', fontsize=16, fontweight='bold')
plt.ylabel('Accuracy', fontsize=12)
plt.xlabel('Epoch', fontsize=12)
plt.legend(['Train', 'Validation'], loc='upper left')
plt.grid(True, linestyle='--', alpha=0.5)
plt.show()
